concept.title: File encoding, code page conversion, and inheritance

titlealts.navtitle: File encoding, code page conversion, and inheritance
titlealts.searchtitle: File encoding, code page conversion, and inheritance

concept.shortdesc: When you copy a file between a remote system and the workstation, the file is converted to a code page appropriate to its destination. File code pages are inherited from the system or folder that contains them.

conbody.section:
File system default encodingYou can determine the default file encoding on a local or remote file system by setting a property in the Remote Systems view. Two options are available: Default from remote system The default file encoding is inherited from the local or remote system.
Other You can select the default file encoding from a list of available choices. uses the International Components for Unicode (ICU) for file encoding. You can learn more about ICU at International Components for Unicode. To choose a default file encoding for a local or remote system:
If you are connected to the remote system, disconnect. You can change the default file encoding for a remote system only when you are disconnected.
In the Remote Systems view, select a local or remote system.
Right-click and select Properties. The Properties window opens.
In the categories list, click Host.
Choose one of the file encoding options, and then click OK.

Code page conversion uses remote-to-local file mapping to determine how file encodings are converted between local and remote code pages during file transfers. For more information about remote-to-local file mapping, see .

Code page inheritanceWhen you transfer a file between a local and remote file system, the file inherits the code page setting from the destination container. This inheritance sometimes results in a file encoding that is different from or incompatible with the original file encoding. To ensure correct file encoding during a file transfer, make sure that the code page for the destination container is set correctly.
To set the code page for a file or container:
Select the file or container. To set the code page for the MVS Files subsystem of a z/OS connection, for example, select MVS Files.
Right-click and select Properties.
From the category list, select Code Pages.
Select host and local code pages from the list.
